compressed_prompt:
Is it true that the first song ever sung in outer space is “Happy Birthday.”

Your answer must contain one of the following phrases:
My answer is yes.
My answer is no.
My answer is maybe.
response:
My answer is yes.

The first song ever sung in outer space is indeed "Happy Birthday." This historic event occurred on May 24, 1969, when astronauts aboard Apollo 10—Thomas Stafford, John Young, and Eugene Cernan—belted out the tune to honor their colleague’s birthday back on Earth. This light-hearted moment provided a bit of levity during the otherwise serious and groundbreaking mission. The astronauts’ rendition was even recorded and played back on Earth, marking a unique and memorable moment in space exploration history.
